rationale: Dhar and Wertenbroch (2000) is cited in the core theory section to define hedonic versus utilitarian products and to support the claim that hedonic options are evaluated more affectively. The citing paper also uses this source in its empirical design, noting that Experiment 3 stimuli were adapted from Dhar and Wertenbroch. This is more than a passing literature-list mention because it contributes to both conceptual setup and operationalization of a key boundary-condition test.
relation_description: The citing paper applies Dhar and Wertenbroch's hedonic-versus-utilitarian framework to examine how self-focused attention changes reliance on feelings. It imports that distinction into hypothesis development and adapts related product-attribute stimuli for Experiment 3.
